channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court upholds a Biden-era rule regulating ghost guns
item.description: The court says ghost guns, which are virtually untraceable weapons that require assembly, are considered "firearms" and subject to ATF regulation.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Trump increasingly asks the Supreme Court to overrule judges blocking key parts of his agenda
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As losses mount in lower federal courts, President Donald Trump has returned to a tactic that he employed at the Supreme Court with remarkable success in his first term.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump commutes the sentence of Ozy Media co-founder Carlos Watson in financial conspiracy case
item.description: A White House official says President Donald Trump has commuted the sentence of Ozy Media co-founder Carlos Watson.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump moved Thursday to end collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration investigates Maine for claims of withholding gender transitions from parents
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — The administration of President Donald Trump is investigating Maine with a claim that dozens of school districts in the state violate federal law by withholding information about students' gender transitioning from parents. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tim Walz calls for 'shadow government' to counter Trump admin; son dunks on former veep nominee
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz on Friday called for a "shadow government" to counter what he said were the Trump administration's lies to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key role as liaison and senior adviser inside the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key position inside the Pentagon as a Department of Homeland Security liaison and senior adviser, Hegseth's office confirmed.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for permission to resume deporting Venezuelan migrants under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday asked the Supreme Court for permission to resume deportations of Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law, while a court fight continues.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deportations under Alien Enemies Act
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union and Democracy Forward sued the Trump administration over its use of the wartime law to quickly deport people, which they say violates due process.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawyers say increased immigration enforcement is filling courtrooms
item.description: It’s been just more than two months since the Trump administration began implementing its plans to deport more people in the country without legal status. And the impact is being felt at the Fort Snelling Immigration Court.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: McConnell: U.S. headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Judiciary calls on Biden DOJ prosecutor to testify in Dr Eithan Haim case
item.description: Dr. Eithan Haim was indicted for exposing Texas Children's Hospital's transgender procedures on minors. The case was dismissed due to the prosecutor's ties to Texas' medical system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fewer Americans now see Canada as a close US ally as Trump strains a longtime partnership
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Americans are less likely to see Canada and the U.S. as close allies than they were two years ago, the latest indication that President Donald Trump's tariff threats and talk of taking over a neighboring ally are souring a critical economic and military relationship. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota doctor: Hospitals in ‘existential crisis’ and potential Medicaid cuts won’t help
item.description: Dr. Rahul Koranne, president and CEO of the Minnesota Hospital Association, joined Cathy Wurzer to talk about what recent job loss and federal volatility mean for the health care of all Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. to slash 10K HHS jobs as part of restructuring plan
item.description: The major restructuring of the Department of Health and Human Services is part of DOGE's cost efficiency initative
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Judge to hear arguments against pro-Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: During Thursday morning's hearing, the federal government is expected to expand on its newest accusations against Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: NPR and PBS heads answer questions on Capitol Hill regarding allegations of bias
item.description: The CEOs of the largest U.S. public broadcasting networks appeared before a House subcommittee chaired by Republican Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman and David Folkenflik, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo talks possible Medicaid cuts, education, movie studios
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo said he may have to call a special session later in the year to handle possible cuts from the federal government that directly impact the state budget.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida 'inmate bill of rights' would require improving prison conditions
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker is fighting for legislation that would protect the rights of prisoners in the state's corrections system.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Florida Democrats continue to push for Medicaid expansion
item.description: Amid fears of federal cuts, the battle continues at the statehouse as Florida Democrats continue to push the GOP super majority to consider expanding Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks three agencies from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday indefinitely blocked the Department of Government Efficiency's access to troves of sensitive personal data from three federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
